LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT TELSTRA INCORPORATED'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT, THE DEMURRER AS TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION IS OVERRULED. THAT CAUSE OF ACTION HAS BEEN ADEQUATELY PLED. THE DEMURRERS AS TO THE FOURTH AND SIXTH CAUSES OF ACTION FOR CLAIMS FOR HARASSMENT BASED ON AGE AND GENDER ARE SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND, BUT WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO PLAINTIFF SEEKING A MOTION FOR LEAVE TO AMEND SHOULD PLAINTIFF OBTAIN FACTS AT SOME POINT THAT WOULD BE SUFFICIENT TO SUPPORT SUCH CLAIMS. REASONABLE DISCOVERY MAY BE CONDUCTED BY PLAINTIFF TO EXPLORE SUCH CLAIMS. PARTIES SHALL PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER TO THE COURT FOR SIGNATURE. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976 (302/HK)

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar May-01-2017 Vacated

MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-08-2017 Off Calendar

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Sep-22-2016 Continued To May-30-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION TO CONTINUE JURY TRIAL
Off Calendar Sep-22-2016 Text Ruling

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-08-2016 Set For Trial On Feb-06-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-07-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. KATHLEEN M. LUCAS, ESQ. of the Lucas Law Firm, Tel No. 415-402-0200 appearing for the Plaintiff;
Atty. ELIZABETH J. BOCA, ESQ. of Epstein Becker & Green, P.C., Tel No. 415-398-3500 appearing for Defendant Telstra Incorporated.

Matter argued and submitted by counsel. The Court modifies its tentative ruling, and orders as follows: As to DEFENDANT TELSTRA INCORPORATED'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT, the demurrer as to the second cause of action is overruled. That cause of action has been adequately pled. The demurrers as to the fourth and sixth causes of action for claims for harassment based on age and gender are sustained without leave to amend, but without prejudice to Plaintiff seeking a motion for leave to amend should Plaintiff obtain facts at some point that would be sufficient to support such claims. Reasonable discovery may be conducted by Plaintiff to explore such claims. Parties shall prepare and submit a proposed order to the Court for signature. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/HK)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-11-2015 Continued To Apr-27-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-18-2015 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-22-2015 Continued To Dec-30-15 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610
